explicit_limitations.impact: The result tests whether the predictor can recover the emulator's hidden generative structure; it does not guarantee the same accuracy on silicon.
explicit_limitations.limitation: Ground-truth runtimes come from a high-fidelity white-box emulator, not from real B200 kernel launches.

explicit_limitations.impact: Generalization outside the studied matrix is unverified.
explicit_limitations.limitation: The validation matrix is synthetic and covers batch, heads, sequence length, head dimension, causal mask, and precision; it omits custom tile sizes, num_splits, fused variants, and real workloads.

explicit_limitations.impact: Actual kernel launch geometry may differ, especially for custom configurations.
explicit_limitations.limitation: Tile sizes and occupancy assumptions are approximations derived from the FA4 paper and Blackwell microbenchmarks rather than measured on a target kernel binary.

explicit_limitations.impact: MAPE on cleaner silicon measurements could be lower or higher depending on unmodeled scheduling effects.
explicit_limitations.limitation: The emulator injects 3% residual noise, so part of the reported MAPE floor reflects the ground-truth generator rather than predictor fidelity alone.

sections.falsification_boundary: If real B200 measurements become available and the combined predictor's MAPE exceeds the useful-improvement threshold (MAPE > 25% or fewer than 75% within 30%), the current claim boundary must be narrowed and the paper must report the transfer-validation result.

supported_claims.claim: The combined white-box predictor achieves 4.50% MAPE on the 160-configuration held-out validation set, compared with 22.22% for the reproduced FlashAttention-4 roofline baseline.
100% of held-out validation configurations are within 30% absolute percentage error and the predicted dominant bottleneck matches the emulator on 100% of configurations.
The TMA/L2 effective-bandwidth correction is the dominant improvement: by itself it reduces MAPE to 5.76% and already places every configuration inside the 30% error envelope.
Occupancy and precision terms provide measurable refinement, pushing the combined model from 5.76% to 4.50% MAPE and reducing max APE from 19.9% to 14.4%.

All correction factors are bounded multiplicative terms with a physical interpretation (hbm_factor 0.85, l2_factor 0.45, tma_factor 0.70, mma_efficiency 0.85, mufu_efficiency 0.85, launch_overhead_us 0.5).
supported_claims.evidence: calibration_params.json in operation set 20260704-130224-experiment-7f255a5e, source code predictor.py and constants.py

unsupported_claims: The predictor achieves 4.5% MAPE on real B200 silicon without silicon validation.
The correction factors transfer unchanged to other GPUs or other attention kernels.
The model captures all implementation-specific scheduling, launch, or power-throttling effects.
The synthetic matrix is representative of production FA4 workloads.

summary: Allowed claims, supporting evidence, falsification boundaries, and explicit limitations for the paper outline.
title: Claim-Evidence Boundary: FA4 B200 White-Box Runtime Predictor
